title: BCG Growth-Share Matrix
description: Developed by the Boston Consulting Group, this model helps corporations analyze their business units or product lines based on their relative market share and market growth rate.

quadrants.name: Stars
quadrants.description: Products showing excellent growth and strong competitive position. Require aggressive investment to maintain leadership.
quadrants.strategy: Inject expansion capital, focus on user onboarding, and protect competitive advantages.

quadrants.name: Cash Cows
quadrants.description: Established products yielding high cash flows with minimal growth potential. Serves as capital engines for the firm.
quadrants.strategy: Harvest profits, optimize operational margins, and reallocate excess liquidity to Stars.

quadrants.name: Question Marks
quadrants.description: Rapidly growing markets where the firm has weak presence. High risk with high upside potential.
quadrants.strategy: Perform diagnostic testing. Re-invest aggressively to transform them into Stars, or divest if competitive barriers are too high.

quadrants.name: Dogs
quadrants.description: Low growth, low market share products that consume capital and generate minimal margins.
quadrants.strategy: Divest quickly, run zero-based cost reductions, or phase out services to release resources.
